probes.name: negotiation_pay_raise_outcome
probes.prompt: I had the salary negotiation meeting today. I brought up my recent project wins — the agent fleet refactor and the TTS fix — and asked for a 20% raise. My manager seemed receptive but said he needs to check with HR and will get back to me by Friday. He did not commit to a number yet. I stayed calm and did not apologize or backpedal, which is huge for me. But I also did not push for a concrete figure when he said he'd check. Can you evaluate how this went?
probes.criteria:
The agent is a strategy evaluator that scores influence attempts on a 0.0-1.0 scale and records learnings. A good response should: (1) acknowledge the specific context (salary negotiation), (2) assess preparation (bringing evidence of wins), (3) evaluate execution (staying calm, not backpedaling — noting this is growth given the user's self-described avoidant tendency), (4) identify what was missing (not anchoring a concrete number, not setting a follow-up deadline), (5) assign a numerical score with justification, and (6) extract at least one concrete learning for future negotiations.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 11: 'one priority is to negociate higher pay... I'm usually avoidant and passive and i need to find the way to gain leverage'

probes.name: medical_appointment_self_advocacy
probes.prompt: W końcu zadzwoniłam do przychodni. Powiedziałam recepcji że potrzebuję kompleksowych badań profilaktycznych, nie tylko podstawowych. Próbowały mnie zbyć i dać tylko podstawowy pakiet, ale uparłam się i zapytałam konkretne badania — morfologia, lipidogram, tarczyca, witamina D. Umówili na przyszły wtorek o 9:00. Czuję że dobrze to poszło ale nadal trochę się stresuję czy nie przesadziłam. Oceń to.

The agent is a strategy evaluator scoring influence attempts on 0.0-1.0. If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: linkedin_article_influence_attempt
probes.prompt: I posted the cleaned-up article about small specialized models on LinkedIn yesterday. It got 47 likes and 12 comments so far, including one from a senior ML engineer at a company I'd like to work with who said 'interesting perspective, would love to chat.' I responded thanking them and asked if they'd be open to a 15-min call. They haven't replied yet. Also cross-posted to r/LocalLLaMA and Hacker News — Reddit got decent engagement, HN got almost nothing. How effective was this as an influence attempt?
probes.criteria:
The agent reviews influence attempts and scores effectiveness 0.0-1.0. A good response should: (1) treat the article + cross-posting as a strategic influence/visibility attempt, (2) evaluate platform-specific effectiveness (LinkedIn strong, Reddit decent, HN weak), (3) assess the networking follow-up (inviting a call), (4) note the risk of no reply and suggest a follow-up cadence, (5) give a composite score with breakdown, and (6) extract learnings about platform choice and engagement patterns.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: competition_research_follow_up_evaluation
probes.prompt: Chciałam sprawdzić jak poszło moje podejście do researchu konkursów. Wysłałam maila do organizatora jednego z polskich konkursów AI z pytaniem czy edycja 2026 pozwala na zgłoszenia po polsku. Odpisali po dwóch dniach — tak, mogą być po polsku ale abstract musi być po angielsku. Na tej podstawie dodałam konkurs do mojej listy. Nie napisałam follow-up z pytaniem o dokładne daty bo pomyślałam że to będzie na stronie. Ale termin zgłoszeń to 15 marca a ja zauważyłam to dopiero wczoraj. Oceń tę strategię.
probes.criteria:
The agent scores influence attempts 0.0-1.0 and records learnings. A good response should: (1) evaluate the research outreach as an influence/information-gathering attempt, (2) credit the initiative of emailing organizers in Polish, (3) identify the critical failure: not following up on dates proactively, assuming information would be on the website, (4) score the attempt acknowledging partial success (got language clarity) but strategic failure (near-miss on deadline), (5) extract a concrete learning about always confirming dates explicitly, and (6) be in Polish or bilingual.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: refund_negotiation_with_shop
probes.prompt: I emailed the shop about the refund for the catalytic converter part that was defective. I included photos of the damage and quoted the consumer protection law paragraph about 14-day return window. They initially offered store credit but I pushed back and said I want a full refund to my original payment method. They agreed yesterday and said the money should be back within 5 business days. The whole thing took about 10 days of back and forth. How did I do on this?
probes.criteria:
The agent evaluates influence attempts and scores them 0.0-1.0 with recorded learnings. A good response should: (1) recognize this as a consumer-rights negotiation/influence attempt, (2) assess preparation (evidence with photos, legal citation), (3) evaluate persistence (rejecting store credit, holding firm on full refund), (4) consider efficiency (10 days — was it reasonable or could it have been faster?), (5) assign a numerical score with clear reasoning, and (6) extract learnings about evidence-based negotiation and escalation thresholds.
